key_insight: MF-PhiBE turns discrete-time transition data into a continuous-time Wasserstein-space policy-evaluation equation for model-free mean-field control.
engineering_relevance: It enables actor-critic learning for McKean-Vlasov systems when the true drift and diffusion are unknown and only sampled transitions are available.
method: Replace unknown infinitesimal drift/covariance with one-step estimators from data.
Preserve the HJB generator structure on probability measures via a continuous-time PDE.
Combine with an entropy-regularized policy-gradient theorem to build a model-free actor-critic scheme.
evidence_signal: The paper proves first-order $O(94t)$ consistency in general and second-order accuracy in the linear-quadratic case, with numerical tests on LQR and a crowd-aversion benchmark.
watch_item: The approach depends on reliable one-step estimators and discretization quality, so performance may degrade when transition data are sparse or noisy.
paper_title: Mean-Field PhiBE: Continuous-Time Mean-Field Reinforcement Learning from Discrete-Time Data
